items.scenario: You are a master's student in environmental science writing your thesis on urban tree canopy. You met with Professor Cho two weeks ago about chapter three. Since then you have hit conflicting findings in two cited studies and are unsure how to reconcile them.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Cho. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the two conflicting findings you encountered.
Explain how you have tried to reconcile them on your own so far.
Ask for guidance on how to present the conflict in the chapter.
items.subject: Question about conflicting sources in chapter three

items.scenario: You are a junior editor at a small publishing house. Your supervisor recently piloted a shared annotation tool for manuscript reviews. After two weeks of using it, you have noticed both real improvements and a few practical issues that the rest of the team should know about.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Lambert. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what has worked well during the first two weeks.
Tell which issues have come up most often in practice.
Suggest a small adjustment to the rollout schedule.
items.subject: Two-week reflection on the shared annotation tool

items.scenario: You bought a refurbished electric kettle from an online appliance store eight weeks ago. The kettle worked fine for the first month, but recently the automatic shutoff has stopped engaging and the base is sometimes warm to the touch. The product is still under its warranty.
items.direction: Write an email to the Warranty Team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how the shutoff problem first appeared.
Explain why you are concerned about safety in your kitchen.
Ask which next steps are required to begin the warranty replacement.
items.subject: Warranty claim for automatic shutoff failure on refurbished kettle

items.scenario: You have volunteered weekly at the Hawthorne community library for the past year, mostly shelving books and helping at the children's reading hour. The library recently lost two staff members and the volunteer coordinator has expanded volunteer roles, but coordination has been a bit chaotic.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Mendoza. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the team for the additional opportunities volunteers have been offered.
Describe the scheduling confusion that has come up over the past month.
Suggest a small change to the weekly sign-up process.
items.subject: Volunteer coordination after the recent staff changes
